page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 24 vCPUs and 48 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the server is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. This processor contains 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supporting two threads per core. The cache hierarchy includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highcpu-24 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, particularly in gzip compression at 31.82 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core tests. It also achieves top-tier results in text generation for small (135M) language models, reaching 433.41 tokens/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 34,445.22 and static web serving throughput of 41.73 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_tile and AMX_int8) for accelerated matrix operations, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a high compute-to-memory ratio, making it a cost-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. However, the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff for data-intensive databases or deep learning training. The server is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, single-threaded compression tasks, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference using small to medium models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 12 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core performance, particularly in gzip compression and static web serving throughput, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, presenting a clear tradeoff for storage-heavy or graphics-accelerated tasks. It is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads, making it well-suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.

Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations
